# Bullet Rust SDK

> Rust SDK for the Bullet perpetual futures DEX. Supports REST, WebSocket, and on-chain transaction signing via Ed25519.

This file provides everything an LLM or agent needs to build a trading bot, integration, or tool on top of the Bullet exchange using the `bullet-rust-sdk` crate.

## Quick Start

```rust
use bullet_rust_sdk::{
    Client, Keypair, Network, Side, MarketId, NewOrderArgs, NewOrderExt, PositiveDecimal,
    ServerMessage, Topic, OrderbookDepth,
};

#[tokio::main]
async fn main() -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
    // 1. Create keypair and client (fetches + caches exchange metadata)
    let keypair = Keypair::from_hex("0x_your_private_key_hex")?;
    let client = Client::builder()
        .network(Network::Testnet) // or Network::Mainnet
        .keypair(keypair)
        .build()
        .await?;

    // 2. Resolve symbol to MarketId (cached, no network call)
    let market_id = client.market_id("BTC-USD").expect("BTC-USD not found");

    // 3. Place a limit buy
    let price = PositiveDecimal::try_from(rust_decimal::Decimal::from(50000))?;
    let size = PositiveDecimal::try_from(rust_decimal::Decimal::new(1, 3))?; // 0.001
    let resp = client.place_orders(
        market_id,
        vec![NewOrderArgs::limit(price, size, Side::Bid)],
        false, // replace
        None,  // sub_account_index
    ).await?;
    println!("TX: id={}, status={:?}", resp.id, resp.status);

    // 4. Cancel all orders on a market
    client.cancel_market_orders(market_id, None).await?;

    Ok(())
}
```

## Architecture

The SDK is a single crate (`bullet-rust-sdk`) that re-exports all commonly-needed types at the crate root. You never need to add internal crates as direct dependencies:

- `bullet-exchange-interface` -- On-chain types (UserAction, Side, OrderType, MarketId, etc). Accessible via `bullet_rust_sdk::*` (flat re-exports) or `bullet_rust_sdk::types::bullet_exchange_interface` (full path).
- `bullet-ws-interface` -- WebSocket message types (DepthUpdate, BookTickerMessage, etc). Access via `bullet_rust_sdk::types::*` or `bullet_rust_sdk::ServerMessage`.

## Dependency

```toml
# In Cargo.toml -- use git dependency with a pinned rev:
bullet-rust-sdk = { git = "https://github.com/bulletxyz/bullet-rust-sdk.git", rev = "...", default-features = false }

# Or path for local development:
bullet-rust-sdk = { path = "../bullet-rust-sdk/rust", default-features = false }
```

The SDK requires `edition = "2024"` and uses `tokio` for async.

## Important Terminology

| Bullet SDK term | Common term | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `Side::Bid` | Buy | SDK uses Bid/Ask, not Buy/Sell |
| `Side::Ask` | Sell | |
| `MarketId(u16)` | Symbol ID | Numeric. Resolve via `client.market_id("BTC-USD")` |
| `PositiveDecimal` | Decimal (>0) | Newtype over `rust_decimal::Decimal`. Use `PositiveDecimal::try_from(decimal)` |
| `OrderType::PostOnly` | Maker order | |
| `OrderType::ImmediateOrCancel` | Market order | Standard across perp exchanges -- market orders are IoC |
| `OrderType::Limit` | Limit order | |
| `CallMessage` | Transaction payload | Only needed for manual `Transaction::builder()` flow |

## Common Imports

```rust
// Convenience methods only (most bots):
use bullet_rust_sdk::{
    Client, Keypair, Network, Side, MarketId, OrderId, ClientOrderId,
    NewOrderArgs, NewOrderExt, CancelOrderArgs, PositiveDecimal, SubmitTxResponse,
    // WebSocket
    ManagedWebsocket, WsEvent, ServerMessage, Topic, OrderbookDepth, KlineInterval,
    // Errors
    SDKError, WSErrors,
};

// Only if using manual CallMessage construction:
use bullet_rust_sdk::{Transaction, CallMessage, UserAction, UserActionDiscriminants};

// Generated REST response types (for type annotations):
use bullet_rust_sdk::codegen::types::{
    Account, Balance, Symbol, BinanceOrder, OrderBook, ExchangeInfo,
};

// WebSocket message types (for pattern matching):
use bullet_rust_sdk::types::{
    DepthUpdate, AggTradeMessage, BookTickerMessage, MarkPriceMessage,
    OrderUpdateMessage, OrderUpdateData,
};
```

## Symbol Resolution

`Client` fetches and caches `exchange_info` during construction. Use `client.market_id()` to resolve symbols — no network call needed after construction.

```rust
// Resolve symbol to MarketId
let market_id = client.market_id("BTC-USD").expect("unknown symbol");

// Get all available symbols
for sym in client.symbols() {
    println!("{}: MarketId({}), precision: {}/{}",
        sym.symbol, sym.market_id.0, sym.price_precision, sym.quantity_precision);
}

// Look up by MarketId
let info = client.symbol_info(MarketId(0)).unwrap();

// Refresh for long-running bots (picks up newly listed markets)
client.refresh_metadata().await?;
```

## REST API Methods

All REST methods are on `Client` (via `Deref` to the generated Progenitor client). Responses are wrapped in `ResponseValue<T>` — call `.into_inner()` to unwrap. The `my_*` convenience methods unwrap automatically.

### Market Data

```rust
// Exchange info (full metadata — usually prefer client.market_id() instead)
let info = client.exchange_info().await?.into_inner();

// Orderbook -- args: (limit, symbol)
let book = client.order_book(/*limit*/ Some(20), /*symbol*/ "BTC-USD").await?.into_inner();
// book.bids: Vec<Vec<String>> -- [[price, qty], ...]
// book.asks: Vec<Vec<String>> -- [[price, qty], ...]
// Prices/qtys are strings; parse into your preferred numeric type:
//   let price: rust_decimal::Decimal = book.bids[0][0].parse()?;

// Ticker price -- args: (symbol)
let price = client.ticker_price(/*symbol*/ "BTC-USD").await?.into_inner();
// price.price: Decimal, price.symbol: String

// Funding rate -- args: (start_time, end_time, symbol)
let rate = client.funding_rate(/*start*/ None, /*end*/ None, /*symbol*/ "BTC-USD").await?.into_inner();
```

### Account Data

```rust
// Using convenience methods (derives address from client keypair):
let account = client.my_account().await?;        // no .into_inner() needed
let balances = client.my_balances().await?;
let orders = client.my_open_orders("BTC-USD").await?;

// Or with explicit address (for querying other accounts):
let address = keypair.public_key_bs58();  // base58 format
let account = client.account_info(&address).await?.into_inner();
let balances = client.account_balance(&address).await?.into_inner();
let orders = client.query_open_orders(&address, "BTC-USD").await?.into_inner();

// BinanceOrder fields:
// { order_id: u64, symbol: String, side: String ("BUY"/"SELL"),
//   price: Decimal, orig_qty: Decimal, executed_qty: Decimal,
//   order_type: String ("LIMIT"/"POST_ONLY"/"IOC"/"FOK"),
//   client_order_id: Option<String>, status: String ("NEW"/"PARTIALLY_FILLED"/"FILLED"/"CANCELED") }
```

## Order Operations

### Order Construction Helpers

```rust
use bullet_rust_sdk::*;

// One-liners: fill in order_type + defaults (reduce_only=false, no client_id, no tpsl)
// (Requires `use bullet_rust_sdk::NewOrderExt;` for the associated fns)
let order = NewOrderArgs::limit(price, size, Side::Bid);
let order = NewOrderArgs::post_only(price, size, Side::Ask);
let order = NewOrderArgs::ioc(price, size, Side::Bid);      // market-order equivalent

// For advanced fields (reduce_only, client_order_id, tpsl), use the struct directly:
let order = NewOrderArgs {
    price, size, side: Side::Bid,
    order_type: OrderType::Limit,
    reduce_only: true,
    client_order_id: Some(ClientOrderId(99)),
    pending_tpsl_pair: None,
};
```

### Convenience Methods (recommended)

These handle CallMessage construction, transaction signing, and submission internally.

```rust
let market_id = client.market_id("BTC-USD").unwrap();

// Place orders
let resp = client.place_orders(market_id, vec![
    NewOrderArgs::limit(price, size, Side::Bid),
], false, None).await?;

// Cancel specific orders
client.cancel_orders(market_id, vec![CancelOrderArgs {
    order_id: Some(OrderId(12345)),
    client_order_id: None,
}], None).await?;

// Cancel all orders on a market
client.cancel_market_orders(market_id, None).await?;

// Cancel all orders across all markets
client.cancel_all_orders(None).await?;

// Amend (cancel + replace) atomically
client.amend_orders(market_id, vec![AmendOrderArgs {
    cancel: CancelOrderArgs { order_id: Some(OrderId(12345)), client_order_id: None },
    place: NewOrderArgs::limit(new_price, new_size, Side::Bid),  // use helpers for the replacement order
}], None).await?;
```

**Important: Order IDs.** `SubmitTxResponse` confirms the transaction succeeded but does NOT contain exchange-assigned `order_id`s. To get order IDs, listen for `ServerMessage::OrderUpdate` on the WebSocket — the `PlaceOrder` variant includes the assigned `order_id`. Alternatively, use `client_order_id` in `NewOrderArgs` to track orders without waiting for the WS event.

### Manual CallMessage Construction (advanced)

For full control over transaction parameters (custom max_fee, explicit signer, etc.):

```rust
use bullet_rust_sdk::{CallMessage, UserAction, Transaction};

let call_msg = CallMessage::User(UserAction::PlaceOrders {
    market_id, orders: vec![NewOrderArgs::limit(price, size, Side::Bid)],
    replace: false, sub_account_index: None,
});

let signed = Transaction::builder()
    .call_message(call_msg)
    .max_fee(10_000_000)        // override client default
    .signer(&other_keypair)     // override client keypair
    .client(&client)
    .build()?;

let resp = client.send_transaction(&signed).await?;
```

## WebSocket Streaming

### Managed WebSocket (recommended)

`ManagedWebsocket` auto-reconnects with exponential backoff + jitter and replays subscriptions after reconnect. It's `Send + Sync` (channel-based, no Mutex needed) and works on both native and wasm32 targets.

Two equivalent factories:

```rust
// Fluent convenience (bon builder)
let mut ws = client.connect_ws_managed().call().await?;

// Or direct — mirrors what the WASM bindings use
let mut ws = ManagedWebsocket::connect(&client).await?;
```

```rust
use bullet_rust_sdk::*;
use bullet_rust_sdk::ws::models::TaggedMessage;

let mut ws = client.connect_ws_managed().call().await?;

ws.subscribe([
    Topic::depth("BTC-USD", OrderbookDepth::D20),
    Topic::book_ticker("BTC-USD"),
    Topic::agg_trade("BTC-USD"),
    Topic::mark_price("BTC-USD"),
], None)?;  // fire-and-forget; server ack arrives as WsEvent::Message

while let Some(event) = ws.recv().await {
    match event {
        WsEvent::Message(msg) => match *msg {
            ServerMessage::DepthUpdate(d) => {
                // Prices/qtys are strings; parse into your preferred numeric type:
                // let price: rust_decimal::Decimal = d.bids[0].0.parse()?;
                // let qty: f64 = d.bids[0].1.parse()?;
            }
            ServerMessage::BookTicker(bt) => {
                // bt.best_bid_price / best_bid_qty / best_ask_price / best_ask_qty are Strings
            }
            ServerMessage::AggTrade(t) => {
                // t.price / t.quantity are Strings
                // t.side: String ("BUY" or "SELL"), t.is_buyer_maker: bool
            }
            ServerMessage::MarkPrice(mp) => {
                // mp.mark_price / mp.index_price / mp.funding_rate are Strings
            }
            ServerMessage::OrderUpdate(ou) => {
                // ou.order: OrderUpdateData enum:
                //   PlaceOrder { common, side, price, quantity, ... }
                //   TradeFill { common, side, price, quantity, last_filled_qty, ... }
                //   Cancel { common, ... }
                // common.order_id: u64 (exchange-assigned)
                // common.status: "NEW" | "PARTIALLY_FILLED" | "FILLED" | "CANCELED" | "EXPIRED"
            }
            ServerMessage::Tagged(TaggedMessage::Pong(_)) => {}
            ServerMessage::Tagged(_) => {} // subscribe acks, etc
            ServerMessage::Unknown(err, raw) => {
                eprintln!("Parse error: {err}, raw: {raw}");
            }
            _ => {}
        },
        WsEvent::Reconnecting => { /* log; subscriptions will replay automatically */ }
        WsEvent::Disconnected(reason) => { eprintln!("{reason}"); break; }
    }
}
```

Configure reconnect behavior:

```rust
use bullet_rust_sdk::ManagedWsConfig;
use std::time::Duration;

let config = ManagedWsConfig::builder()
    .max_retries(10)
    .initial_backoff(Duration::from_millis(500))
    .channel_capacity(50_000)
    // Force reconnect if no server message arrives in this window. Defaults
    // to 60s; set Duration::ZERO to disable (not recommended for prod).
    .idle_timeout(Duration::from_secs(30))
    // Reset exponential backoff to initial after the connection has been up
    // for this long — prevents "zombie accept-then-drop" servers from being
    // hammered at initial_backoff forever. Default 30s.
    .backoff_reset_after(Duration::from_secs(60))
    .build();

let mut ws = client.connect_ws_managed().config(config).call().await?;
```

### WebSocket Order Submission

Orders can also be submitted via WebSocket for lower latency:

```rust
let signed = Transaction::builder()
    .call_message(call_msg)
    .client(&client)
    .build()?;

// Handles base64 encoding internally. Fire-and-forget — the send is queued
// to the background task; server acks arrive as WsEvent::Message.
ws.place_order(&signed, None)?;
ws.cancel_order(&cancel_signed, None)?;

// If you already have a base64 string:
// ws.order_place(&b64_tx, None)?;
// ws.order_cancel(&b64_tx, None)?;
```

### Raw WebSocket (advanced)

`WebsocketHandle` gives direct socket access but requires manual reconnection. It is `Send` but NOT `Sync`. Use `ManagedWebsocket` for production bots.

### Managed WebSocket errors

`ManagedWebsocket` calls return `Result<(), ManagedWsError>`:
- `Stopped` — background task has terminated (disconnected or handle dropped)
- `Busy` — the internal command channel is full. Indicates a stuck task; treat as backpressure, retry with a small delay, or reconnect.

## Error Handling

```rust
use bullet_rust_sdk::{SDKError, WSErrors};

// REST errors
match client.order_book(/*limit*/ Some(10), /*symbol*/ "BTC-USD").await {
    Ok(resp) => { /* use resp.into_inner() */ }
    Err(e) => {
        let sdk_err: SDKError = e.into();
        match &sdk_err {
            SDKError::ApiError(resp) => {
                // resp.status: u16, resp.message: String, resp.details: Option<Value>
                if resp.is_retryable() { /* 429 or 5xx — backoff and retry */ }
            }
            SDKError::HttpError(_) => { /* network error, retry */ }
            SDKError::SchemaOutdated => { /* SDK version mismatch, update SDK */ }
            SDKError::MissingKeypair => { /* no keypair configured on client */ }
            _ => { /* non-retryable */ }
        }
        // sdk_err.is_retryable() works on all variants
    }
}
```

## Schema Validation

The client validates that the local SDK schema matches the remote exchange schema on `Client::builder().build()`. If the exchange has been updated and the SDK is out of date, you'll get `SDKError::SchemaOutdated`.

To restrict validation to only the actions you use (avoiding false failures from unrelated schema changes):

```rust
use bullet_rust_sdk::UserActionDiscriminants;

let client = Client::builder()
    .network(Network::Mainnet)
    .user_actions(vec![
        UserActionDiscriminants::PlaceOrders,
        UserActionDiscriminants::CancelOrders,
        UserActionDiscriminants::CancelMarketOrders,
    ])
    .build()
    .await?;
```

## Available OrderType Variants

| Variant | Value | Use Case | Helper |
|---|---|---|---|
| `Limit` | 0 | Standard limit order | `NewOrderArgs::limit(price, size, side)` |
| `PostOnly` | 1 | Maker-only, rejected if it would take | `NewOrderArgs::post_only(price, size, side)` |
| `FillOrKill` | 2 | Fill entirely or cancel | Use `NewOrderArgs { .. }` struct literal |
| `ImmediateOrCancel` | 3 | Fill what you can, cancel rest. Market order equivalent | `NewOrderArgs::ioc(price, size, side)` |

(`limit` / `post_only` / `ioc` live on `NewOrderExt` — `use bullet_rust_sdk::NewOrderExt;`.)

## Available WebSocket Topics

| Topic | Constructor | Data |
|---|---|---|
| Orderbook depth | `Topic::depth("BTC-USD", OrderbookDepth::D20)` | `ServerMessage::DepthUpdate` |
| Best bid/ask | `Topic::book_ticker("BTC-USD")` | `ServerMessage::BookTicker` |
| Trades | `Topic::agg_trade("BTC-USD")` | `ServerMessage::AggTrade` |
| Mark price | `Topic::mark_price("BTC-USD")` | `ServerMessage::MarkPrice` |
| Candles | `Topic::kline("BTC-USD", KlineInterval::H1)` | `ServerMessage::Kline` |
| Liquidations | `Topic::force_order("BTC-USD")` | `ServerMessage::ForceOrder` |
| All tickers | `Topic::all_tickers()` | Mini-ticker for all symbols |
| All mark prices | `Topic::all_mark_prices()` | Mark price for all symbols |

`OrderbookDepth` variants: `D5`, `D10`, `D20`.
`KlineInterval` variants: `M1`, `M5`, `M15`, `M30`, `H1`, `H4`, `D1`.

## Parsing Prices and Quantities

REST and WS responses return prices/quantities as strings (Binance convention). The SDK does not opine on a numeric type — parse into whatever you use:

```rust
use rust_decimal::Decimal;

// WS depth update (PriceLevel is a tuple struct of two Strings)
let price: Decimal = depth.bids[0].0.parse()?;
let qty: Decimal   = depth.bids[0].1.parse()?;

// WS book ticker (Strings on the struct)
let bid_px: Decimal = bt.best_bid_price.parse()?;

// WS trade (Strings on the struct)
let trade_px: f64 = trade.price.parse()?;

// REST BinanceOrder.side is a String "BUY" / "SELL" — match or map as needed
let side = match order.side.as_str() {
    "BUY" => Side::Bid,
    "SELL" => Side::Ask,
    _ => panic!(),
};
```

## Networks

| Network | REST URL | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `Network::Mainnet` | `https://tradingapi.bullet.xyz` | Production |
| `Network::Testnet` | `https://tradingapi.testnet.bullet.xyz` | Paper trading / testing |
| `Network::from("https://custom-url")` | Custom URL | Self-hosted or staging |
